description: Full 52-week muscle-building program, Upper/Lower split, 4 training days/week. Three mesocycles: 1) Foundation Hypertrophy 8–12 (wks 1–18), 2) Strength-Hypertrophy 5–8 heavy / 6–10 (wks 19–36), 3) Metabolic/Intensity 10–20 with shorter rest (wks 37–52). Nine 5–6 week blocks, each ending in a deload. Per-muscle effective-set volume held constant at Moderate-tier MAV-low landmarks across all blocks; auxiliary hip abductor/adductor work by request; no advanced techniques. Loads self-managed via rep targets.

exercises.notes: First press of the session — leave about 2 reps in the tank early on; never let form break to chase a number. RIR drops week to week (eases off W1-2, near-hard W5), then back well off in the W6 deload.
Keep technique tight; stop the set when reps start slowing. Work harder each week toward the last build week, then deload W6.
Keep technique tight; stop the set when reps start slowing. Work harder each week toward the last build week, then deload W6.
Smooth, controlled reps. Earlier sets leave 1-2 in reserve; the last set of the block weeks can reach failure. W6 deload: stay well short of failure.
Light and strict — lead with the elbows, no swinging. Chase the burn, not the weight. Last reps should be tough; ease off in the W6 deload.
Light and strict — lead with the elbows, no swinging. Chase the burn, not the weight. Last reps should be tough; ease off in the W6 deload.
10-15 reps throughout (single-joint arm work). Control the lowering; last set near failure on build weeks, easy in W6.
10-15 reps throughout (single-joint arm work). Control the lowering; last set near failure on build weeks, easy in W6.
Full stretch at the bottom, brief pause at the top, full range every rep.

Hinge from the hips with a long spine; stop the set the moment back position is threatened. Keep 2 in reserve on build weeks.

Auxiliary finisher (you requested abductor work). Push knees out against the pad, controlled tempo.
Auxiliary finisher (you requested adductor work). Full stretch, squeeze legs together under control. No other adductor-primary option exists in the library.
Round the spine to crunch; keep hips fixed so the abs do the work, not the hip flexors.
